examples.title: Myth Detection Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to identify potential software myths in project documentation
examples.code:
PROCEDURE DetectMyths(projectDocs)
 mythsFound ← []
 FOR EACH doc IN projectDocs
 IF Contains(doc, "add more programmers") THEN
 mythsFound.Add("Management Myth: Brooks' Law violation")
 END IF
 IF Contains(doc, "requirements frozen") THEN
 mythsFound.Add("Customer Myth: Requirements stability assumption")
 END IF
 IF Contains(doc, "can be tested in") THEN
 mythsFound.Add("Practitioner Myth: Quality testing assumption")
 END IF
 IF Contains(doc, "estimate is exact") THEN
 mythsFound.Add("Management Myth: Schedule certainty assumption")
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN mythsFound
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates how to systematically check project documentation for common software myths, helping project reviewers identify and address misconceptions early.

examples.title: Project Health Check Based on Iron Triangle
examples.description: Algorithm to evaluate project balance using Iron Triangle constraints
examples.code:
PROCEDURE CheckProjectHealth(scope, time, cost)
 optimalScore ← CalculateOptimalBalance()
 currentScore ← (scope * time * cost)^(1/3)
 
 IF currentScore > optimalScore * 1.2 THEN
 RETURN "WARNING: Project is over-constrained"
 END IF
 
 IF scope > scopeBaseline * 1.5 THEN
 RETURN "RISK: Scope creep detected"
 END IF
 
 IF time > timeBaseline * 1.3 THEN
 RETURN "RISK: Schedule overrun"
 END IF
 
 IF cost > costBaseline * 1.3 THEN
 RETURN "RISK: Budget overrun"
 END IF
 
 RETURN "Project within acceptable parameters"
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This algorithm helps project managers monitor Iron Triangle balance and detect when one constraint is being pushed beyond acceptable limits.
